rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ni-catalyzed α-allylation/alkylation of β-ketoesters with alkenes (e.g., 1,1-diphenylethylene or allyl-TMS) via radical-polar crossover at the anode.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of a Ni(II)-enolate complex generates a carbon-centered radical/cation that adds to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2 (and TMS-related byproducts), closing the paired electrolysis.

key_intermediates.name: C-C bond formed Ni(II) alkyl intermediate C' (after alkene addition with allyl-TMS 2k)
β-silyl carbocation D' (after second SET oxidation)

electron_flow: Ni(II) coordinates the β-ketoester enolate (A'); single-electron anodic oxidation gives an α-carbon radical (B') that adds to the alkene to form alkyl–Ni intermediate C'; a second anodic SET produces β-silyl cation D', which loses TMS+ (as TMSOH/TMS2O) to give product 31, while the cathode evolves H2.

probe_reagents.name: no current (control)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3 formed, confirming the reaction is electrochemically driven.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not detected; TEMPO-adduct 50 isolated in 82% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 368.1831), supporting a C-centered radical at the α-position of 1a.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 not detected; BHT-adduct 51 isolated in 75% yield (HRMS [M+Na]+ 431.2183), corroborating the radical intermediate.

notes: Mechanism proposed (Scheme S3) is for the allyl-TMS variant giving product 31; the α-radical is generated via anodic oxidation of a Ni(II)-enolate rather than direct substrate oxidation. Cathodic chemistry includes H2 evolution and degradation of TMS group (TMSOH, TMS2O). No DFT or CV reported in the provided pages.
